name: Sport & Games
description: Sports facts, rules, famous athletes, and game knowledge — from the rugby field to the Olympic track!
author: Game Night Crew

questions.question: In rugby, a converted try is worth how many points in total?
questions.explanation: A try is worth 5 points and the conversion kick adds 2 more, giving you 7 points total. Rugby 101!

questions.question: How many players are on a soccer team on the field at one time?
questions.explanation: 11 players per side, including the goalkeeper. If you got this wrong, you might need to watch more Bafana Bafana!

questions.question: Which country has won the most FIFA World Cup titles?
questions.explanation: Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup 5 times (1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, 2002). The Seleção are the only team to have played in every single World Cup!

questions.question: What sport is played at Newlands Cricket Ground in Cape Town?
questions.explanation: Newlands is one of the most beautiful cricket grounds in the world, nestled under Table Mountain. A proper Cape Town gem!

questions.question: In basketball, how many points is a shot from beyond the three-point line worth?
questions.explanation: A shot from beyond the arc is worth 3 points. That's why players like Steph Curry changed the game!

questions.question: Which SA rugby team is known as the Bulls?
questions.explanation: The Bulls are based at Loftus Versfeld in Pretoria. Blue Bulls through and through!

questions.question: In tennis, what is a score of 40-40 called?
questions.explanation: When both players reach 40, it's called deuce. You then need to win two consecutive points to take the game!

questions.question: Who captained the Springboks to victory in the 2019 Rugby World Cup?
questions.explanation: Siya Kolisi became the first Black captain to lead the Springboks to World Cup glory. A historic moment for SA rugby and the nation!

questions.question: In cricket, what does 'LBW' stand for?
questions.explanation: Leg Before Wicket — when the ball hits the batsman's leg and the umpire judges it would have hit the stumps. One of cricket's trickiest rules!

questions.question: Which South African swimmer won gold at the 2016 Olympics in the 400m freestyle?
questions.explanation: Trick question! Mack Horton of Australia won the 400m freestyle in 2016. Chad le Clos won his Olympic gold in the 200m butterfly in 2012!

questions.question: What is the nickname of the South African men's national cricket team?
questions.explanation: The Proteas — named after South Africa's national flower, the King Protea. A beautiful name for a team with a complex but proud cricket history!

questions.question: In which year did South Africa host the FIFA World Cup?
questions.explanation: South Africa hosted the 2010 FIFA World Cup — the first African nation to do so. Remember the vuvuzelas? The whole world heard them!

questions.question: How many Grand Slam tournaments are there in tennis each year?
questions.explanation: Four Grand Slams: Australian Open, French Open, Wimbledon, and the US Open. Winning all four in one year is called a Calendar Grand Slam!

questions.question: Which position in rugby wears the number 10 jersey?
questions.explanation: The flyhalf wears 10 — the playmaker who controls the game. Think Handré Pollard or Morne Steyn slotting those crucial kicks!

questions.question: Arrange these cricket dismissal types from MOST to LEAST common in professional cricket: (1) Bowled (2) Caught (3) Run out (4) LBW
questions.explanation: Caught is by far the most common (over 50%), followed by LBW, then bowled, then run out. Catching wins matches — every coach knows that!

questions.question: Which South African city is home to the Kaizer Chiefs soccer club?
questions.explanation: Kaizer Chiefs are based in Johannesburg — one of the most supported clubs in Africa. Amakhosi! Their rivalry with Orlando Pirates (the Soweto Derby) is legendary!

questions.question: What does a yellow card mean in soccer?
questions.explanation: A yellow card is a caution/warning. Two yellows in the same match equals a red card, and you're off! Better behave after that first one.

questions.question: Wayde van Niekerk holds the world record in which track event?
questions.explanation: Wayde van Niekerk ran 43.03 seconds in the 400m at the 2016 Rio Olympics — from lane 8! A South African legend who broke Michael Johnson's 17-year record!

questions.question: In a game of pool (8-ball), what must you sink last to win?
options: Your last striped or solid ball
questions.explanation: You must pot all your balls (stripes or solids) and then sink the 8-ball to win. Sink the 8-ball early and you lose! Now rack 'em up!

questions.question: How many holes are played in a standard round of golf?
questions.explanation: A standard round of golf is 18 holes — 9 on the front nine and 9 on the back nine. South Africa has some of the most beautiful courses in the world!

questions.question: The Comrades Marathon is run between which two South African cities?
questions.explanation: The Comrades Marathon is an ultramarathon of about 90km between Durban and Pietermaritzburg, alternating direction each year. The ultimate test of human endurance!

questions.question: Which Springbok holds the record for most Test tries scored?
questions.explanation: Bryan Habana holds the record with 67 tries in 124 Tests. The 'Speed Machine' was virtually uncatchable on the wing!

questions.question: What is the highest possible break in a frame of snooker?
questions.explanation: A maximum break of 147 is achieved by potting 15 reds with 15 blacks, then all the colours. It's snooker perfection and incredibly rare!

questions.question: Which country does Formula 1 driver Max Verstappen race for?
questions.explanation: Max Verstappen races under the Dutch flag for the Netherlands, though he was born in Belgium. He's dominated F1 in recent years with Red Bull Racing!

questions.question: In T20 cricket, how many overs does each team face?
questions.explanation: T20 stands for Twenty20 — each team gets 20 overs. It's fast, furious, and perfect for a night at the stadium with a cold drink!

questions.question: What is the name of the famous rivalry match between Kaizer Chiefs and Orlando Pirates?
questions.explanation: The Soweto Derby is one of the biggest football derbies in Africa and the world. When Chiefs and Pirates play, the whole of South Africa stops to watch!

questions.question: Which sport uses a shuttlecock?
questions.explanation: Badminton uses a shuttlecock (also called a birdie). The shuttlecock can travel over 400 km/h in a smash — making it the fastest racquet sport!

questions.question: How many times have the Springboks won the Rugby World Cup?
questions.explanation: The Springboks have won the Rugby World Cup 4 times — 1995, 2007, 2019, and 2023. Back-to-back champions and the most successful team in World Cup history!

questions.question: In darts, what is the highest possible checkout with a single dart?
questions.explanation: The bullseye (50 points) is the highest you can score with one dart as a checkout. You must finish on a double, and the bullseye counts as double 25!

questions.question: Rank these sports by the number of players on a team (on field/court), FEWEST to MOST: (1) Basketball (2) Rugby Union (3) Water Polo (4) Cricket
questions.explanation: Basketball: 5, Water polo: 7, Cricket: 11, Rugby union: 15. From 5 to 15 — quite the range in team sizes!

questions.question: Which golfer is known as the 'Big Easy' and hails from South Africa?
questions.explanation: Ernie Els is nicknamed the 'Big Easy' for his smooth, effortless swing. A two-time Open champion and one of SA's greatest sporting exports!

questions.question: What is the distance of a standard marathon?
questions.explanation: A marathon is 42.195 km (26.2 miles). The distance was standardised at the 1908 London Olympics. A half marathon is 21.1 km!

questions.question: In the Monty Hall problem (from the game show), you pick a door, the host opens another showing a goat. Should you switch?
options: Yes, switch — you have a 2/3 chance of winning
No, stay — you had the best odds first
questions.explanation: Switching gives you a 2/3 chance of winning! Your initial pick had 1/3 probability. When the host reveals a goat, the other door absorbs the remaining 2/3. Counter-intuitive but mathematically proven!

questions.question: Which South African cricketer was known as 'Mr 360' for his ability to hit the ball anywhere?
questions.explanation: AB de Villiers earned the nickname 'Mr 360' for his incredible shot-making ability — he could hit the ball to any part of the ground. A true genius of the game!

questions.question: In chess, which piece can only move diagonally?
questions.explanation: The bishop moves only diagonally, any number of squares. Each player starts with two bishops — one on light squares, one on dark. They never switch colour!

questions.question: Which country has hosted the Olympics the most times (Summer and Winter combined)?
questions.explanation: The United States has hosted the Olympics the most times — including Los Angeles (1932, 1984, 2028), Atlanta (1996), St. Louis (1904), plus multiple Winter Games!

questions.question: What is a 'hat-trick' in cricket?
questions.explanation: A hat-trick is taking 3 wickets on 3 consecutive deliveries. The term actually originated in cricket — a bowler who did this was traditionally awarded a new hat!

questions.question: Caster Semenya is a South African Olympic champion in which event?
questions.explanation: Caster Semenya won Olympic gold in the 800m at the 2012 and 2016 Games. A proud South African champion whose career has been marked by both brilliance and controversy.

questions.question: In which sport would you perform a 'scrum'?
questions.explanation: A scrum is a rugby set piece where the forwards from each team bind together and push against each other to win the ball. Eight against eight — pure power!

questions.question: Rank these poker hands from WEAKEST to STRONGEST: (1) Full House (2) Flush (3) Straight (4) Four of a Kind
questions.explanation: Straight < Flush < Full House < Four of a Kind. The ranking is based on rarity — a four of a kind is much harder to get than a straight. Know your poker hand rankings!

questions.question: Which South African hosted the most iconic moment in Rugby World Cup history, catching the trophy in a Springbok jersey in 1995?
questions.explanation: Nelson Mandela wearing François Pienaar's number 6 Springbok jersey at the 1995 World Cup final is one of the most iconic moments in sport. It united a nation!

questions.question: What does 'FIFA' stand for?
questions.explanation: FIFA stands for Fédération Internationale de Football Association — founded in 1904 in Paris. It governs football worldwide with 211 member associations!

questions.question: Which South African boxer became the undisputed cruiserweight champion of the world?
questions.explanation: Baby Jake Matlala was actually a flyweight champion! This was a tricky one. But he remains one of SA's most beloved boxers despite his small stature.

questions.question: The 'Sardine Run' along the KZN coast sometimes coincides with which sporting event calendar?
questions.explanation: The Midmar Mile, the world's largest open water swimming event, takes place in KZN in February. The Sardine Run is typically June-July, but both are iconic KZN events!

questions.question: In which city is the iconic FNB Stadium (Soccer City) located?
questions.explanation: FNB Stadium (Soccer City) is in Johannesburg — it hosted the 2010 World Cup final and is the largest stadium in Africa with a capacity of about 94,000!

questions.question: What is the term for scoring zero runs in cricket?
questions.explanation: Scoring zero is called a 'duck' — supposedly because the zero on the scoreboard looks like a duck's egg. A golden duck is being out first ball without scoring!

questions.question: Which South African rugby player is nicknamed 'The Beast'?
questions.explanation: Tendai 'The Beast' Mtawarira earned his nickname for his incredible strength as a loosehead prop. His destruction of Phil Vickery in the 2009 Lions series was legendary!

questions.question: The Durban July is South Africa's most famous event in which sport?
questions.explanation: The Durban July (now Hollywoodbets Durban July) is South Africa's premier horse racing event, held annually at Greyville Racecourse. It's as much about fashion as it is about racing!

questions.question: Which chess piece can jump over other pieces?
questions.explanation: The knight is the only chess piece that can jump over other pieces. It moves in an L-shape — two squares in one direction and one square perpendicular!

questions.question: In the 2023 Rugby World Cup final, the Springboks beat which team 12-11?
questions.explanation: The Springboks beat the All Blacks 12-11 in a nail-biting final in Paris. All of SA's points came from Handré Pollard's boot — no tries needed!

questions.question: Which country invented the sport of rugby?
questions.explanation: Rugby originated at Rugby School in England in 1823, according to legend when William Webb Ellis picked up the ball during a football match and ran with it. The World Cup trophy is named after him!

questions.question: Jacques Kallis is widely regarded as one of the greatest all-rounders in cricket history. What did he excel at?
questions.explanation: Jacques Kallis was a world-class batsman AND bowler — he scored over 13,000 Test runs and took nearly 300 Test wickets. Arguably the greatest all-rounder ever!

questions.question: What sport is associated with the term 'albatross'?
questions.explanation: An albatross in golf means scoring 3 under par on a single hole (e.g., a 2 on a par 5). It's extremely rare and one of the most exciting things that can happen on a golf course!

questions.question: Which South African stadium is nicknamed 'The Bullring'?
questions.explanation: Loftus Versfeld in Pretoria is known as 'The Bullring' — home of the Blue Bulls. Playing there at altitude in the Highveld heat is one of the toughest away trips in rugby!

questions.question: Rank these by the number of possible legal game states (FEWEST to MOST): (1) Tic-Tac-Toe (2) Chess (3) Checkers (4) Go (19x19 board)
questions.explanation: Tic-Tac-Toe: ~5,478 states. Checkers: ~5 x 10^20. Chess: ~10^47. Go: ~2 x 10^170 (more than atoms in the universe!). Go's complexity is why AI took so much longer to master it than chess.

questions.question: In which year did Bafana Bafana win the Africa Cup of Nations for the first time?
questions.explanation: Bafana Bafana won their first and only AFCON title in 1996, hosted on home soil — beating Tunisia 2-0 in the final at FNB Stadium!

questions.question: How many consecutive Test matches did the Springboks win on their record-breaking streak from 1997 to 1998?
questions.explanation: The Springboks won 17 consecutive Tests from 1997 to 1998 under coach Nick Mallett — a world record that stood until 2024!
